security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract's capacity to handle high volume of vote transactions is limited.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to disrupt voting by spamming the contract.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Can disrupt voting affecting governance, but requires attacker resources.

assumptions: The Governance Smart Contract (Component 9) or its interaction layer (Data Flow 11) has limitations on transaction processing capacity.
An attacker has sufficient resources ($BERA for gas) to generate a high volume of vote transactions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement transaction rate limiting at application layer or rely on blockchain's gas model.

description: An attacker could flood the Governance Smart Contract (Component 9) with vote transactions via Data Flow 11, potentially causing congestion or disrupting the voting process.

attack_surface.data_description: Signed Vote Transaction with vote details

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract fails to verify user's voting eligibility (ownership/delegation of Asset 4).

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to cast votes they are not eligible for.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Allows manipulating governance outcomes with illegitimate votes.

assumptions: The Governance Smart Contract (Component 9) has a vulnerability in its logic for verifying voting eligibility based on Asset 4 holdings/delegation.
An attacker can craft a transaction exploiting this vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Robustly verify voting eligibility based on Asset 4 holdings/delegation within the Governance Smart Contract logic.

description: An attacker could exploit a vulnerability in the Governance Smart Contract (Component 9) to cast votes without the required Asset 4 voting power via Data Flow 11, influencing governance illegitimately.
